title: Rolling Expanding Window Z-Score Feature Normalization
summary: ML models (especially gradient boosting and neural networks) are sensitive to feature scale. If raw values like RSI (0–100) and price change percent (e.g. 0.02) are fed as-is, the model may over-weight the numerically larger feature. Rolling z-score normalization standardises each feature relative to its own recent history using an expanding window (or rolling 252-bar window), computing z = (x - rolling_mean) / rolling_std. This is data-leakage safe because at each point in time it only uses data available up to that point — unlike StandardScaler which normalises on the full training set. A 2025 Medium study on z-score trading strategies showed that normalised signals produce more statistically consistent entry thresholds across different volatility regimes. The hybrid trading paper (ArXiv 2601.19504) explicitly used StandardScaler on all features before XGBoost and achieved 63% out-of-sample accuracy vs baseline.
research_topic: Win Rate Improvement — ML Feature Engineering & Predictive Signal Construction
expected_impact: +2–3% win rate via reduced scale bias in ML model
deferral_reason: not in paper_trading_single_account Active pipeline — deferred for future stage review
